question.text: Under UBO (Ultimate Beneficial Ownership) requirements, what is the controlling interest threshold for identifying beneficial owners in a company?

answer.text: More than 10% of shares, capital, or profits
More than 25% of shares, capital, or profits
More than 5% of shares, capital, or profits
More than 15% of shares, capital, or profits

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For companies, a beneficial owner is identified when a person holds more than 10% of shares, capital, or profits (directly or indirectly). This threshold applies to companies (excluding individuals and listed companies which are exempt). **The Trap:** The 10% threshold for UBO identification is lower than many candidates expect. It captures a broader set of beneficial owners for enhanced transparency. **Cross-Exam Context:** UBO requirements are part of the broader KYC/AML framework. RTAs must identify UBOs for non-individual MF investors (corporates, trusts, partnerships). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When onboarding corporate clients, be prepared for UBO documentation requirements. The 10% threshold means even minority shareholders of the investing company may need to be disclosed, adding to the paperwork.
